meta.title: Pseudomonas sp. strain KC represents a new genomovar within Pseudomonas stutzeri
meta.journalTitle: International Journal of Systematic and Evolutionary Microbiology

abstractHtml: Pseudomonas sp. strain KC (=ATCC 55595=DSM 7136) is a denitrifying aquifer isolate that produces and secretes pyridine-2,6-bis(thiocarboxylate) (PDTC), a chelating agent that fortuitously transforms carbon tetrachloride without producing chloroform. Although KC has been used successfully for full-scale bioremediation of carbon tetrachloride, its taxonomy has proven difficult to resolve, as it retains properties of both Pseudomonas stutzeri and Pseudomonas putida. In the present work, a polyphasic approach was used to conclude that strain KC represents a new genomovar (genomovar 9) within the species P. stutzeri.
